Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Boomloom: Think with your hands 114 upvotes, 10 comments
  2. The paddle wheel aircraft carriers of Lake Michigan 83 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. My first patch to the Linux kernel 69 upvotes, 8 comments
  4. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 178 upvotes, 33 comments
  5. Show HN: Termcraft – Terminal-first 2D sandbox survival in Rust 113 upvotes, 20 comments
  6. Common Lisp Development Tooling 88 upvotes, 17 comments
  7. Some things just take time 680 upvotes, 205 comments
  8. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 177 upvotes, 50 comments
  9. Bayesian statistics for confused data scientists 111 upvotes, 29 comments
  10. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 255 upvotes, 85 comments
  11. Grafeo – A fast, lean, embeddable graph database built in Rust 220 upvotes, 76 comments
  12. Sashiko: An agentic Linux kernel code review system 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Hide macOS Tahoe's Menu Icons 200 upvotes, 71 comments
  14. Trivy ecosystem supply chain briefly compromised 67 upvotes, 23 comments
  15. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 252 upvotes, 123 comments
  16. Do Not Turn Child Protection into Internet Access Control 667 upvotes, 355 comments
  17. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 478 upvotes, 283 comments
  18. Alpha Micro AM-1000E and AM-1200 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  19. Thinking Fast, Slow, and Artificial: How AI Is Reshaping Human Reasoning 143 upvotes, 77 comments
  20. Linking Smaller Haskell Binaries (2023) 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  21. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 148 upvotes, 90 comments
  22. Chest Fridge (2009) 100 upvotes, 61 comments
  23. How Ford burned $12B in Brazil (2021) 54 upvotes, 29 comments
  24. It's Their Mona Lisa 24 upvotes, 10 comments
  25. How Invisalign became the biggest user of 3D printers 161 upvotes, 118 comments
  26. Show HN: Time Keep – Location timezones, timers, alarms, countdowns in one place 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Ubuntu 26.04 Ends 46 Years of Silent sudo Passwords 369 upvotes, 361 comments
  28. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 11 upvotes, 5 comments
  29. Training Center for Maneuvering on Manned Model Ships 11 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Cross-Model Void Convergence: GPT-5.2 and Claude Opus 4.6 Deterministic Silence 16 upvotes, 10 comments
